programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Erica is jealous of Barrys performance when they both get jobs at Spencers; Murray insists that Adam get a job, but Beverly thinks he should focus on filmmaking.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When a talent agent tells Adam hes only suitable for `nerd roles, Beverly makes it her mission to turn him into a heartthrob. Barry decides to embrace a punk lifestyle, and Murrays attempt to get more involved in Ericas life backfires.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam and the other Adam Goldberg have a karate match to settle once and for all a long-running feud. Erica and Geoffs relationship goes public.

programme.title: Dont Tell the Bride: Revisited
programme.desc: Jai wed Leanne, whos scared of heights, 100 feet in the air in 2016. Now its his turn to squirm as he sits down with his bride and her dad to relive their nuptials.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The guys share their biggest fears when they crash Jays annual hiking trip. Back home, the girls get their DIY on as they build Haleys new nursery.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil feels emasculated when Bill wows Dylan and Luke with heroic tales. He gets them over for some male bonding, but it goes rather wrong.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short clip from Rude Tubes naughty little sister Rude-ish Tube, featuring food and animals.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Los Angeles to visit La Frite - a family-owned French restaurant being run into the ground by Andre Ramillion, his son and daughter Celine and Alex, and head chef Martine.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam is terrified when he and his friends are recruited to the school baseball team. Geoffs father interferes in his relationship with Erica.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Erica and Valley Erica find a new band member to replace Lainey. Adam and Murray plot to escape from Valentines Day dinner with Beverly.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Alexs company puts her up at a new luxury apartment building with a number of high-profile residents that the family is eager to take advantage of. Phil is on a mission to review the restaurants famous sliders for his food blog.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil visits his dad after he hears some worrying news. Meanwhile, Jay takes Claire and Mitch on a stroll down memory lane.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge and Homer rent an apartment in the Waverly Hills school district after Springfield Elementary is voted as the worst school in the state. Bart quickly establishes a new bad boy image in attempt to impress his new fellow students. He also tells everyone that Lisa and pop star Alaska Nebraska are best friends in order to boost her popularity. Meanwhile, Homer enjoys the bachelor lifestyle in their new Waverly Hills abode.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: Four Great Women and a Manicure
programme.desc: The female members of the Simpson household make a trip to a nail salon and discuss whether a woman can be successful without a man at her side. Marge and Lisa each tell their own parodies of 4 classical stories, including Snow White and the Seven Dwarfs.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: An outbreak of food poisoning at Krustys restaurant chain is traced back to tainted barley in nearby Ogdenville, settled years earlier by Norwegian immigrants. Ogdenville Barley Mills is promptly shut down and Springfield finds itself swamped by the unemployed.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Pasadena, California to visit a Jamaican restaurant called Cafe Kingston. There, Gordon finds Dr Una Morris, a micro-managing owner who also works as a radiotherapist in a clinic next door to the restaurant. She is determined to use hospital techniques in her kitchen and is constantly sealing everything into plastic bags. Dr Morris is as stubborn as she is proud. Can Gordon help?

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Eagle Rock California, to visit Capri - a restaurant in a rut - owned by overgrown identical twins, Jim and Jeff. The twins could have walked straight out of Alice in Wonderland and their method of running a restaurant is just as fantastic. Half comedy act half cry-babies, Jim and Jeff seem oblivious to the grime and dust riddling their restaurant.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Ned opens a Christian amusement park, but is disappointed when the tame religious attractions prove unpopular, but then visitors start to have visions.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons win a holiday but Homer refuses to pay the airport tax and decides the family should travel by goods train. On the train, they meet a hobo who regales them with his own versions of classic American tales, starring the Simpsons as, among others, Paul Bunyan, Connie Appleseed and Tom Sawyer.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay reluctantly takes Phil on a model-airplane excursion, and Gloria goes shopping with Alex. Meanwhile, Claire and Manny get to know one another as siblings.

programme.title: Modern Family

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Mediterranean Adventure
programme.desc: The bikers begin their Sardinian adventure in the small town of Cagliari, where everyone is in high spirits for the feast of St Efisio. The locals are dressed in traditional garb to parade through the town on decorated bullock carts. The bikers join in the fun by cooking a local speciality, almond and orange cakes.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond cooks for her always-hungry teenage sons. She makes buffalo chicken totchos, a fun twist on pizza with her broccoli cheese Stromboli and a one-pot beef noodle skillet.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond prepares speedy seafood done four ways. She makes coconut shrimp and mango dipping sauce Greek grilled salmon with veggies, and a mixed seafood pasta.

programme.title: Jamies One-Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver shows us how to make a foolproof traybaked salmon, super-easy tomato fritters, and roasted rolled pork with a risotto that are both cooked in the same casserole pan.

programme.title: Jamies One-Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver takes midweek meals up a notch, as he knocks out flavour-packed gnocchi and giant spicy meatballs, and turns a family favourite on its head with his upside-down fish pie.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina is making dinner for her friend Miguel Florres-Vianna who is out at the barn photographing the garden. While Miguel takes photos he creates a surprise table setting for Ina inspired by foliage found in the garden. On the menu are Panko crusted salmon, warm French lentils, all American cheeseboard and chicken noodle soup for Miguels journey back to the city.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Its a drill dinner for local fire fighters. After a practice fire drill, Ina pitches in and serves the fire fighters up a delicious dinner back at the station. Theres Beef Barley Soup, Firefighter Scott Elleys Sloppy Joes served with Inas Stilton Dressing and make-your-own Pecan Caramel Sundaes.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: In the third series, the search for Britains best amateur baker is back as 12 passionate baking fans compete for the title. Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins return as presenters, as do judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood, who devise the three challenges faced by the competitors each week. They incorporate a signature bake, which tests the bakers personality and creative flair; a technical bake, which tests experience; and a showstopper bake, during which the bakers are able to showcase their depth of skill and talent. Berry and Hollywood inspect, prod and sample the baked goodies as they decide who gets to have their cake - and eat it.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the moment of truth as the victorious four must put their finest hours on a plate for those who served both on the front line and the home front, such as D-Day veterans Ken Sturdy and George Batts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Scotlands top chefs, Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee, and Graham Campbell - attempt to impress this weeks veteran judge. Having missed out last year, returning finalist Jak is determined to make it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scottish chefs Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee and Graham Campbell fight it out for the fish course. Graham is in a confident mood but returning finalist Jak and self-taught Jimmy are pushing him hard. Will the pressure prove too much for ambitious Graham?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scottish chefs Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee and Graham Campbell are battling to plate up a perfect main course for this weeks veteran chef. Self-taught Jimmy is honouring his mum with his rendition of her Chinese beef stew.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Today its the Scottish chefs last chance to impress, with the dessert course. Only two can cook for the judges tomorrow, and with one chef in the lead after a triumphant main, the pressures on for the other two to plate up perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Scottish chefs cook their entire menus again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by WI Calendar Girl Angela Baker, who will help decide which chef will go forward to the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the south wests finest chefs - Jude Kereama, Josh Eggleton and Dominic Chapman - attempt to impress a veteran judge with their starters. As the only newcomer in the kitchen, Jude is determined to plate up a winning dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three south west chefs fight it out for the fish course. Despite schoolboy errors during the starter, experienced Dominic is determined to get a high score on the course. But will newcomer Jude, a seafood master, overcome his nerves to plate up?.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course as the three south-west chefs go head to head. Newcomer Jude is brimming with determination and confidence as the main course battle hits the kitchen.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The south-west chefs have one last chance to impress as they face the dessert course. Only two chefs can go through to cook for the judges; with everything to play for, who will plate up perfection and who will be sent packing?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining south-west judges must cook their entire menus again for judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Joining the judges is Radhika Bynon, a member of the Forest Gate Womens Institute, a modern urban group of women.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Stephen Gomes, Adam Bannister and Phil Carmichael are challenged to plate up perfect starter courses for this weeks veteran judge. Its the first time in the Great British Menu kitchen for all three chefs, so who will triumph and who will fall?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Stephen Gomes, Adam Bannister and Phil Carmichael fight it out for the fish course. Experimental chef Stephen is determined to prove himself with a fish course honouring the WIs tradition of preserving, but hes up against stiff competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course with the Welsh chefs going head to head. With all three chefs paying homage to Welsh lamb; Adam with a refined faggot, Phil with a dish dedicated to his Mum and Stephen with a lamb dish celebrating the WI.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the tricky dessert course and the last chance for the Welsh chefs to secure their place in tomorrows regional final. With everything to play for, the pressure is fierce. Only two can make it through, and one will leave the competition for good.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Two remaining Welsh chefs cook their four courses again for the judges and Rosemary Bishton. One chef will be chosen to represent Wales in the National Finals taking them a step closer to cooking at the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Northern Irelands top chefs - Chris McGowan, Danni Barry and Ben Arnold - go head to head with their starter courses to impress this weeks veteran judge. Former champion Chris is back for glory and determined to reach the banquet, but hes up against two newcomers with their sights firmly set on victory.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chris McGowan, Danni Barry and Ben Arnold are cooking their fish courses. Ambitious Ben is determined to steal Chriss regional crown, but under fierce scrutiny from this weeks formidable veteran, will the pressure to deliver spell melt down?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course for three of Northern Irelands finest chefs - Chris McGowan, Danni Barry and Ben Arnold. Ambitious Ben is out to prove himself with a perfect lamb main course.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the Northern Irish chefs are all striving for one of the two spots in the Regional Final. The pressure is intense, and in the end one chef must leave the competition empty handed.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North Irish chefs are cooking all four courses again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by a guest judge - award winning food writer Felicity Cloake.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mini Patel, Michael OHare and Tim Allen are going head to head this week for a chance to represent the North East region in the National Finals. Today its a battle for starter course, and the first chance to impress their formidable veteran chef.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fight for the fish course, as three of the North Easts finest chefs - Mini Patel, Michael OHare and Tim Allen - are out to impress. After his risky starter caused a stir yesterday Michael is planning another controversial dish in honour of the WIs support of womens rights.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the North Easts most dynamic chefs - Mini Patel, Michael OHare and Tim Allen - go head to head cooking their main courses. Michelin starred Tim is super keen to bag the first ten of the week, but Michael and Mini are hot on his heels.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the North East chefs last chance to impress with the dessert course. Its been a week of highs and lows, modern audacious dishes and classic masterpieces but with only two places up for grabs cooking for the judges, at the end of today who will leave the competition empty handed?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook all four courses again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by a guest judge - WI president Kirsty Bowen from Sheffield - who will help decide which chef will go through to represent the North East in the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Representing the North West are Eve Townson, Matt Worswick and returning contender Mark Ellis. Todays the starter course, and after failing to cook for the judges last year Marks out to redeem himself but he faces stiff competition in executive chef Eve and young gun Matt.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course, as three of the North Wests finest chefs - Eve Townson, Matt Worswick and Mark Ellis - go head to head. Eve and Matt are hoping their classic approach to cooking fish will win the day whilst Mark is going all out with fishy take on a traditional dessert recipe.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the battle for the main course for the three North West chefs - Eve Townson, Matt Worswick and Mark Ellis. Mark remains determined and focused on his battle to get to judges chamber this year, but as the pressure to plate up perfection reaches fever pitch, will all three chefs get a dish to the pass?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the North West chefs last chance to impress, with the dessert course. Only two can cook for the judges tomorrow. Its the end of the road for one chef, but who will it be?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs cook their entire menus again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by award winning food writer Sabrina Ghayour and together theyll decide which chef will go on to represent the North West in the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the starter course for three of London and the South Easts hottest chefs - Matt Gillan, Lee Westcott and Marcus Wareings protege Mark Froydenlund. programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The London and South East chefs - Matt Gillan, Lee Westcott and Marcus Wareings protege Mark Froydenlund - go head to head with their fish courses. Lee is hoping his modern plate of mackerel and passion fruit will outclass Matt and Marks more traditionally inspired salmon dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course for three of London and the South Easts finest chefs - Matt Gillan, Lee Westcott and Mark Froydenlund. Returning contender Matt is determined to bring home a 10 with his ambitious celebration of the billy goat but has he bitten off more than he can chew?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the London and South East chefs are striving to secure one of the two spots in tomorrows Regional Final. The scores are close, so its all to play for, as today marks the end of the competition for one man.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and South East chefs are cooking all four courses again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the Central Regions finest chefs - Pip Lacey, Richard Bainbridge and Jason Hodnett - attempt to impress this weeks veteran judge with their starters. programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three Central Region chefs - newcomer Pip Lacey, 4th time competitor Richard Bainbridge and returning chef Jason Hodnett - fight it out for the fish course. Pip is hoping to outclass her rivals with a smoked haddock dish inspired by her Grandma.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course with three Central Region chefs - Pip Lacey, Richard Bainbridge and Jason Hodnett. Once again Jason is taking risks with his unusual collection of ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the last chance for the Central Region chefs to impress, with the dessert course. Only two chefs can go through to cook for the judges so with everything to play for, who will plate up perfection and who will be sent packing?

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: By early 1944, Hitler had long been expecting an Allied attack on north-western France, but his problem was knowing when and where it would come. This episode takes a look at the Allied operations Bodyguard and Overload.

programme.title: Devils Brigade
programme.desc: Modern soldiers get lessons in hand-to-hand combat and the basics, taught by a 60-year-old Canadian Army Commando.

programme.title: Devils Brigade
programme.desc: The training continues with rock-climbing, weapons-training and target practice; those who remain practise an assault on a bridge.

programme.title: Devils Brigade
programme.desc: The troops learn the history of booby traps and German mines, get some parachute-training at 11,000 feet, and hear from the FSSF veterans.

programme.title: Operation Carpetbagger: The Secret Mission to End World War II
programme.desc: Chronicling Operation Carpetbagger, one of the most top-secret missions of World War II.

programme.title: Operation Oblivion
programme.desc: In the darkest days of World War II in the Pacific, an elite British military team sought to facilitate espionage and sabotage behind enemy lines. This now famous group, the British Special Operations Executive or MI6, faced a critical obstacle.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Concentrates on Hitlers attempts in the latter end of 1944 to shift the balance of power back in his favour.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: The war in the Pacific against Japan was a savage hand-to-hand, island-to-island struggle, fought by new specialist fighting units like the valiant Chindits.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Allied forces converge on Germany, uncovering the horrors of Belsen and Buchenwald, before pushing towards Berlin, where Hitler clings to power in his last desperate days.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: With Germany beaten, the Allies turned their attention to Japan, who made them pay for every inch of gained ground. How was this last, great foe overcome?

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: In the run-up to September 1939, political and economic chaos in the aftermath of World War I allowed Hitler in Germany, Mussolini in Italy and ultra-nationalists in imperial Japan to seize absolute power.

programme.title: Ghost Ships
programme.desc: Investigating underwater mysteries and long-forgotten wrecks in the Graveyard of the North Atlantic. Following professional salvage diver Tony Sampson and his team as they brave the shifting currents and bone-chilling legends of Nova Scotia.

programme.desc: In this film, Michael Palin answers questions on polar exploration, history and travel.

programme.title: The Rise of Napoleon Bonaparte A Military Prodigy
programme.desc: Napoleon Bonaparte, born in 1769 in Corsica, came from minor nobility and claimed Roman heritage.

programme.title: Secrets and Mysteries of Christopher Columbus
programme.desc: Investigating the true origins of Christopher Columbus through historical records and testimony from experts and Columbus living relatives.

programme.desc: Explorer Ernest Shackleton and his team become stranded for nearly two years in severe antarctic conditions. Narrated by Liam Neeson.

programme.desc: Ryan emphasizes natural history, Shackletons determination, and his fathers leadership.

programme.title: Winston Churchill

programme.title: River Hunters

programme.title: River Hunters

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Cheltenham, Britains most Regency town, Professor Alice Roberts attends a period society ball, investigates social hierarchy and explores the Regency fondness for enemas.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Belfast, the UKs most Victorian city, Alice Roberts visits the shipyards that built the Titanic, gets locked up in the Crumlin Road gaol and learns about 19th-century etiquette.

programme.desc: Chris Ward sets out to salvage a light aircraft sitting on the seabed. Jeremy Birch battles to save two vessels that have fallen prey to brutal ice conditions.

programme.title: Deep Water Salvage
programme.desc: Bundles of tree trunks are smashed apart, releasing thousands of logs on the shoreline. Jeremy and his crew must work fast to recover the cargo.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Marden Henge: The communal sweat lodges and feasting remains that illuminate the lost rituals of Stonehenge. Durotriges: A glimpse into the bizarre animal sacrifice rituals offered to their gods by a mysterious Celtic tribe of the first century BC.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the most exciting recent archaeological finds in the east of Britain. A team unearths a mass grave, divers search the Thames for clues to a 17th-century tragedy, and a metal detectorist makes the find of a lifetime.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson is out to discover why Pembrokeshire feels so English. There are castles everywhere and it boasts one of the countrys finest cathedrals, sure signs that the Normans stamped their mark all over this little slice of Wales.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson visits the British Channel Islands, which were occupied by Nazi Germany for five years between 1940 and 1945.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts celebrates the biggest and best archaeological discoveries of 2018 from the north of the UK, starting with a prehistoric Pompeii at the Black Loch of Myrton.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: We join a team as they undertake the largest maritime investigation since the Mary Rose and reveal the extraordinary story of HMS Invincible.

programme.desc: Arctic conditions continue to wreak havoc in British Columbia, while Jeremy Birch is called upon to raise a concrete-hulled vessel.

programme.title: Deep Water Salvage

programme.title: SEX: A Bonkers History

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tonys walk takes him back to 1940 when the sleepy county of Dorset became the frontline in the war against Hitler. His five-day, 60-mile walk along the Jurassic coast reveals the countys hidden World War II story. Starting by the defences on Chesil Beach, Tonys journey encompasses stunning scenery and amazing acts of ingenuity and bravery as he heads east to finish in Swanage and Studland Bay.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson goes for a walk through some of Britains beautiful and historic landscapes. Tony kicks off the series with a 40-mile walk through the glorious Peak District, along the Derwent Valley, where the worlds industrial revolution was born. This is a journey that reveals how Britain transformed itself from a nation of farmers into the industrial powerhouse of the world.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Marden Henge: The communal sweat lodges and feasting remains that illuminate the lost rituals of Stonehenge. Durotriges: A glimpse into the bizarre animal sacrifice rituals offered to their gods by a mysterious Celtic tribe of the first century BC.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the most exciting recent archaeological finds in the east of Britain. A team unearths a mass grave, divers search the Thames for clues to a 17th-century tragedy, and a metal detectorist makes the find of a lifetime.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Walsall in the Midlands looking around a 1950s semi; Martin Roberts meets a man developing a ground-floor one-bedroom flat in Ilford in Essex; and Lucy Alexander catches up on a multi-million-pound redevelopment in East Horsley in Surrey where one small cottage with a big garden is converted into eight separate properties.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house with a bathroom layout challenge in Ripley, Derbyshire, a huge three-storey house with its freehold on offer in Erdington, Birmingham, and a mid-terrace house in Londons East Ham in need of a major makeover are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Lincolnshire looking round a pair of rural semi-detached cottages which the new owner may convert into one home. Martin Roberts is in Cheshire at a bungalow with bags of potential, and Lucy Alexander is in Kent talking to friends embarking on their first project together.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house in Chesterton in Staffordshire has a smelly problem and needs a total overhaul. The new owner of a semi in Bestwood in Nottingham also has a lot to do, while the new owner of a flat in Strawberry Hill has less than a week to sort out her auction buy before she jets off to Germany.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A husband and wife duo add their design skills to a house in Croydon in Surrey, while a seasoned developer creates a high-end house in multiple occupation in Coventry and a first time developer also does a great job with a house full of period features in Nutall in Nottinghamshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets one of two young brothers busy building a property portfolio in Bolsover, Derbyshire, while Dion Dublin checks out a three-bedroom semi in Wolverhampton, and Lucy Alexander is impressed by plans to convert a two-bedroom, fire-damaged terraced house in Londons Plaistow into a smart five-bedroom property.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin has a hair-raising experience at a barbers in Rochdale, Lancashire, while Martin Roberts looks round a great bungalow in Alsager, Cheshire, and Lucy Alexander sees all the potential in a piece of land in Eccles in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a house in Otterton, Devon, which is given a stunning transformation to match the views from the windows, Lucy Alexander looks round a small one-bedroom flat in Tottenham, London, and Dion Dublin peers into a building in Alvaston, Derby, which is in a dangerous state of disrepair.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An end-terrace house in Alfreton in Derby bought by two brothers, a mid-terrace on the coast at Seaham in County Durham and a large three-storey property with a flat and shop in Taylorstown in the Rhondda Valley are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin talk to the new owners to find out what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts falls in love with a quirky cottage in Malvern bought by a builder and his wife, Dion Dublin talks to a first-time property developer who sees her end-terrace house in Chell in Stoke-on-Trent as something for her pension pot, and Lucy Alexander meets a divorced couple who have decided to form a new union as property developers doing up a shop and flat in Dover, Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large piece of land in Ratby in Leicestershire, a terrace in Chesterton in Staffordshire and a 1930s semi with a huge garden and huge potential are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the developers hoping to breathe new life into their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bedroom detached house in Basford in Nottingham, a top floor flat in Portsmouth and a large Edwardian terrace in Crewe are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear about their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A multimillion-pound development in Farningham in Kent, an ambitious, total renovation project in Aspatria in Cumbria and a very small studio flat in Wallington in Surrey are all sold under the hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the new owners to discuss their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A Victorian terrace in Londons Greenwich, two flats in Chatham in Kent and a riverside inn near Great Yarmouth in Norfolk are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners and hear plans for their renovation projects.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three-bedroom house in Borrowash in Derbyshire, another three-bedder in West Bromwich in the Midlands and a quirky four-bedroom house on the Isle of Sheppey are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Pegswood in Northumberland, a terrace in Swadlincote in Derbyshire and a former shop in Chatham in Kent are given makeovers.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy meets a young couple expecting a baby who have to weigh up whether to live in their auction buy in Sydenham in London while Martin talks to a man and his father in law who have bought their first property together in Trentham near Stoke and Dion meets a man who only went into property development after he retired. His latest buy is a three-bed semi in West Bromwich.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house in Wales, a basement office in central London with a huge price tag and two flats in Bootle near Liverpool are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.desc: Joining Alan at Manor Farm are TVs biggest Taskmaster Alan Davies and wildlife enthusiast Michaela Strachan to provide the chat alongside Annabelle Apsion and Cliff Parisi, Poplars favourite duo, as they chat about whats next for Fred and Violet - we also meet a couple who have spent the past five years setting up a hedgehog hospital in their back garden and sample some female-owned beverages that are sure to raise spirits on a spring morning.

programme.title: ITV News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news.

programme.title: James Martins Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is back in the kitchen and is joined by singer and presenter Jane McDonald, chefs Judy Joo and Curtis Stone, and master mixologist Merlin Griffiths.

programme.title: Raymond Blancs Royal Kitchen Gardens
programme.desc: Raymonds journey takes him to the impressive Hampton Court Palace - walking in the shadows of the imposing building, Raymond finds its wonderful walled gardens and chats to Hilary, the Keeper of the Walled Garden.

programme.title: ITV News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news.

programme.title: Mens Six Nations LIVE
programme.desc: Jill Douglas presents coverage from the 2026 Guinness Mens Six Nations as Ireland take on Scotland in Dublin - the final round of this years championship kicks off at the Aviva Stadium as Scotland look to end their run of 11 consecutive defeats against Ireland on Super Saturday.

programme.title: Celebrity Lingo
programme.desc: The hit daytime format returns with a celebrity version - hosted by the inimitable Emmy Award-winning US entertainer RuPaul Charles, Celebrity Lingo sees famous faces pair up and go head-to-head in the fast-paced wordplay game as they vie to fill in the blanks and find the missing words. The celebrity teams will be playing for big money for their chosen charities up against the clock and their opponents.

programme.title: ITV Evening News
programme.desc: The latest headlines from around the world. Followed by a national weather forecast.

programme.title: ITV News London
programme.desc: The latest news in London and the South East, plus local weather.

programme.title: The Chase: Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts a celebrity edition of the hit quiz show. Four well-known celebrities have to work together to try and win thousands of pounds for charity.

programme.title: Wheel of Fortune
programme.desc: Graham Norton invites barrister Daniel, site manager Lorraine, and software sales rep Nicole to take on the iconic Wheel and tackle a series of puzzles for a shot at winning 50,000 pounds.

programme.title: Mens Six Nations LIVE
programme.desc: Mark Pougatch is joined by Jonny Wilkinson, Dan Biggar and Benjamin Kayser for coverage from the Stade de France as the curtain comes down on the 2026 Guinness Mens Six Nations with France entertaining England in Paris - England fans were hoping pre-tournament that this game would be a Grand Slam decider, but painful defeats to Scotland and Ireland have shattered that dream.

programme.title: ITV News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news.

programme.title: Itll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: Comedian Tom Allen uncovers hilarious cock-ups featuring the nations favourite TV shows and stars - expect behind-the-scenes bloopers from our favourite entertainment, documentary and comedy programmes. Caught up in the chaos are Greg Davies, Vernon Kay, Ant and Dec, Mel Giedroyc, Alan Carr, Judi Love, Richard Madeley, Guz Khan and many more while the cock-ups include Steve Backshall being distracted during filming by some rather windy gorillas...

programme.title: English Football League Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the days games in the Football League including Coventry City v Southampton, Middlesbrough v Bristol City, Leicester City v Queens Park Rangers, and Millwall v Blackburn Rovers in the Championship.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo! Mystery Incorporated
programme.desc: A sleepy little village, Crystal Cove boasts a long history of ghost sightings, poltergeists, demon possessions, phantoms and other paranormal occurrences. The renowned sleuthing team of Fred, Daphne, Velma, Shaggy and Scooby Doo prove all of this simply is not real and, along the way, uncover a larger mystery that will change everything.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo! Mystery Incorporated
programme.desc: Shaggy and Scooby win a dinner date with their hero, Vincent Van Ghoul. What they do not realise is that they are unwitting participants in Van Ghouls new reality television show where he has dinner with people, then scares them out of their wits. When a monster known as Nightfright locks the three up and terrorises them, it is up to Fred, Daphne, and Velma to solve the mystery and rescue their friends.

programme.title: Be Cool, Scooby-Doo!
programme.desc: Daphne leads the gang to visit an ancient Blake family castle in Ireland where there is an angry, rampaging giant!

programme.title: Be Cool, Scooby-Doo!

programme.title: The Only Way Is Essex

programme.title: Scooby-Doo! Mystery Incorporated
programme.desc: Velma is feeling like a fifth wheel. Fred has Daphne, and Shaggy has Scooby. Who does she have? So, when a mysterious mer-woman named Amy contacts Velma on her blog, she decides that maybe this will be her special friend.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo! Mystery Incorporated
programme.desc: Creepy Spooky Terror Land theme park is besieged by a manticore, an ancient beast with the body of a dragon, the face of a man and the tail of a scorpion. The mayor begs the gang to help him solve this mystery and get rid of the manticore before the theme park goes out of business.

programme.title: Be Cool, Scooby-Doo!
programme.desc: The gang head out to a Day of the Dead festival. There, they learn the spirit of the old, legendary criminal, El Bandito, has returned to terrorise the locals.

programme.title: Be Cool, Scooby-Doo!

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmondson offers his unique insights on some `Guinness World Records including the worlds shortest and longest cats, the highest waterfall jump, and the longest underwater kiss.

programme.title: The Only Way Is Essex
programme.desc: Dan is rehearsing on the ice with his skating partner Vanessa while the rest of the crew are in sunny Bali - when his mum visits the ice rink to show her support, Dan tells her that hes missing Ella. Emotions run high when Dan and Ella Facetime and Ella confirms that she feels the same way and wants to come home, but Dan convinces her to stay and have fun with everyone before they are reunited for some quality time together.

programme.title: The Only Way Is Essex
programme.desc: Love is in the air as the Bali trip comes to a close and Jordan surprises Sophie with a lavish romantic date, but will Sophie think a proposal is on the cards? Elsewhere, long-distance lovers Dan and Ella enjoy a virtual date, and Harry and Freddie have their first proper date together and discuss whats next. After a tumultuous trip, Joe asks Junaid a very important question. Will this lead to the boys time in Bali ending on a high note?

programme.title: The Only Way Is Essex
programme.desc: The Bali gang are back in Essex and everyone is reunited. Dan and Ella are delighted to be together again and head off for a romantic getaway. Elsewhere, Joe and Junaid are over the moon to be boyfriend and boyfriend and share their happy news with Joes parents. Saff bids an emotional farewell to Jason as she finally moves out, and Elma returns and catches up with Courtney, sharing all the latest about her relationship with Sammy.

programme.title: The Princess Diaries
programme.desc: Princess opens up about the press backlash following the release of the first series - as news stories continue to circulate about her parents, Princess decides to take matters into her own hands. After gaining second place in Tatler magazines Best Dressed List, Princess rushes to find an outfit for the event and looks to mum Katie for fashion advice - she also catches up with friend Heidi Katona as they discuss life with famous parents, fake friends and her current relationship status.

programme.title: The Princess Diaries
programme.desc: Princess decides that its time to step out of her comfort zone - with thoughts of moving out of home, she goes house-hunting with friend Sofia. Elsewhere, Princess considers rejoining the world of dating and starts to set up a dating profile, but she is shocked by what she uncovers - later, after visiting brother Junior in a recording studio, Princess reveals her dreams of pursuing a music career too and, prompted by her dad, signs up for singing lessons.

programme.title: The Floor
programme.desc: From 81 players down to the final nine - after weeks of fierce competition, it all comes down to this. In the grand finale of the game show hosted by Rob Brydon, the remaining contestants face one last series of high-stakes duels - two players will compete in a best-of-three final, but only one of them will conquer The Floor and take the 50,000-pound jackpot home.

programme.title: Britains Got Talent
programme.desc: Following the search for Britains next best talent act. The show features singers, dancers, comedians, variety, and unusual talents of all kinds as they compete for a cash prize and a chance to appear at the Royal Variety Performance.

programme.desc: The sequel to `Madagascar, in which New York Zoo animals, Alex the Lion, Marty the Zebra, Melman the Giraffe and Gloria the Hippo, still stranded on Madagascar, start to leave the island. All of a sudden, they land in the wilderness of Africa, where Alex meets the rest of his family, but has trouble communicating with them after spending so much time at the Central Park Zoo.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. Its themes - love, passion, deception, greed and intrigue - are ones which everyone can relate to.

Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. Its themes - love, passion, deception, greed and intrigue - are ones which everyone can relate to.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason and Miller tackle tribal prejudice after an attempt is made on a local farmers life. Elsewhere, David is terrified about the possible supernatural consequences of Peggys double-dealings.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The village is targeted by a group of violent robbers, Mason and Rachels careers are in jeopardy because of their romantic past, and a familiar friend bids farewell to the village.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Martin and Louisa go out on their first date and she seizes the opportunity for a romantic kiss, but the clumsy doctor manages to ruin the moment with an inappropriate remark. Meanwhile, Louisas friend Hollys condition takes a turn for the worse when she has an allergic reaction to morphine, and Martin has to act quickly to save her life. Louisa is full of admiration for him - and he surprises her by proposing.

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Portwenn is buzzing with the news of Martin and Louisas engagement - but Aunt Joan is disappointed that she has to hear about the impending nuptials from the village postman rather than her own nephew. Elsewhere, Beth Sawles wound isnt healing and Martin discovers that her sister has been treating her with a homemade potion - which proves to be the cause of an outbreak of aspergillosis in the village.

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: A series of death notices in the Oxford Mail, each with a cryptic message, provides clues to Endeavours final investigation, while Thursdays past catches up with him.

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Frost investigates the armed robbery and later, murder of a pawnbroker and begins to suspect one of the victims children of the crime.

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Amused at the attention showered on Inspector Blake by new canteen girl Molly, Stan and Jack tease their superior.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Wycliffe is summoned to trace a farmer who has disappeared and discovers murder at the heart of a farming community struggling to survive.

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A schoolgirl is found dead in a science lab after a school disco. Through her family and friends, Wycliffe and the team set out to discover whether suicide or murder has taken place.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When Andrew Crompton, amateur astronomer and Master of Gresham College, is found dead at the foot of the University Observatory stairs, Lewis and Hathaway find that the finger of suspicion points at all the staff - from tutors right down to the head porter and the college scouts. Lewis uncovers a college-wide blackmail plot and is confused by a mysterious astronomical conundrum.

programme.title: Agatha Christies Marple
programme.desc: While staying with a recently widowed friend, Miss Marple makes the acquaintance of charming young drifter Mike Rogers, then events take a sinister turn when Mike and his new wife Ellie move into the cursed Gipsys Acre.

programme.title: Rosemary and Thyme
programme.desc: Laura hopes to heal a rift with her daughter Helena when she is invited to construct a memorial garden near Helenas home, but then a recovering alcoholic is found dead at the site.

programme.title: Rosemary and Thyme
programme.desc: An old friend of Rosemarys invites the pair to restore the 100-year-old gardens of her French villa before it is opened to the public. However, not everyone is in favour of the impending public access as the duo discover when their hosts husband is found dead and valuable silverware goes missing.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Gated estate Challis Court is a tight-knit community for retired police officers, but when a new arrival is found dead, the other residents regard the murder as an affront. Barnaby realizes that the killer is likely an ex-police officer.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Pastry chef Chrissie Larktons mill has been in her family for centuries. She restored it and turned it into a thriving online business, but the popularity has overshadowed Lower Blissingham, and many of the villagers are not happy about it.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The residents of Elverton-Cum-Latterly battle in the Dominoes Competition that dates back decades, but when the event occurs the same night as a drag queen fundraiser for a terminally ill teenager, it results in a deadly clash of cultures.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual Midsomer Regatta is brought to a standstill when a body is found in the river. It turns out to be that of a noted ladies man who had been previously seen arguing with friends. As Barnaby and Scott dig into the luxury lifestyles of the rowing set, they discover that money problems and sexual jealousy are rife.

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: With her caustic wit and singular charm, DCI Vera Stanhope and her trusted right-hand man face a series of captivating murder mysteries set against the breathtaking Northumberland landscape.

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: False passports and murder draw Inspector Jack Frost and his new assistant into a tragic case of lust, greed and death.

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Olive is furious when Arthur flirts with a brassy-looking conductress at a busmens darts match, then fails to come home.

programme.title: Never the Twain

programme.title: Hamburger Hill
programme.desc: An emotional and harrowing re-creation of the battle to secure Hill 937 in the Ashau Valley during the Vietnam War. The film follows a platoon through eleven agonising assaults over a ten-day period, suffering rivers of mud, stifling heat and driving rain as they battle against a well-entrenched enemy. This searing account of one battle can be seen as a microcosm of the Vietnam War itself.

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Northern Ireland
programme.sub-title: Who Wants to Live Like That?
programme.desc: There have been many false dawns in the conflict, but finally the desire for change propels the possibility of peace for the next generation. Following years of talks at a government level, paramilitary ceasefires are announced on both sides. And when the Good Friday Agreement is finalised in April 1998, there is optimism about new beginnings. But conflict has exacted a heavy cost, and its effects on victims and survivors are lasting and significant.

programme.title: The Moody Blues - Days of Future Passed Live
programme.desc: Celebrating the 50th anniversary of their landmark album, Days of Future Passed, The Moody Blues took to the road to perform the entire LP live. An electric evening at The Sony Centre, Toronto, where the band was joined on stage by a full orchestra for the first time ever, is the definitive performance of this seminal album.

programme.title: Coldplay - Austin City Limits
programme.desc: The Austin City Limits Music Festival is an annual music festival that takes place at Zilker Park in Austin, Texas. The festival features more than 130 bands on eight stages for three days.

programme.desc: The Fillmore Auditorium is a historic concert hall in San Francisco, California. Founded by Bill Graham, it was named after being located originally on the corners of Fillmore Street and Geary Boulevard.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Its the last outing for antiques dealers Roo Irvine and David Harper, and they are on a whistle-stop tour of the Lake District, battling each other, and the elements, in a sporty classic car. Davids hunt for antiques unearths a secret map issued by the RAF, but with time ticking before the auction, Roo gambles on a Georgian longcase clock. David learns about the Cambrian sport of hound trailing, which sees dogs running for miles with only the scent of aniseed to guide them.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Theres a piratical mood for antiques experts Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott as they set sail on their final outing together. This prized piece belonged to Andys grandfather, who obtained it by chance after the Second World War, but the minute hand is missing, the dial is chipped and the clockwork mechanisms have been cobbled together.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Woodwork expert Will Kirk is enchanted by a musical jewellery box that miraculously survived a devastating house fire. Pamela Lamb from Devon cherishes this memento, as it belonged to her elder sister Vera, who sadly passed away as a teenager. Pamela still remembers happy times when they would play with the box, but the fire scarred the pretty painted box.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: First to test the Repair Shops technical prowess, Julianna Smith from Milton Keynes. She is hoping Mark Stuckeys mastery of electronics can bring a radio system, handmade by her late father, back to life. Julianna remembers the radiogram filling the house with rock and roll music as a child. Her dear father, Patrick, was a clever chap and this creation, built from scratch, was his pride and joy. With no instruction manual for this unique item, Mark takes a slow and steady approach.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life. The first item to arrive is a double challenge for metal man Dominic Chinea and upholstery expert Sonnaz Nooranvary. Father and daughter Joe and Sophie Davies have a piece of their family trades history that they are hoping to revive to be used again. Joes father, Bill, opened a barbers shop in 1949, and both Joe and Sophie have followed in his footsteps. One of the last remaining mementos of the original shop is a 1960s chrome and vinyl barbers chair, which Sophie dreams of having as the centre piece of her own shop. Dom must work out how to repair the hydraulic mechanism, while Sonnaz has to replace and reupholster the ripped and faded vinyl seat covering. Next through the barn doors is Sima Oskoui, with an unusual instrument that will test the skills of Julyan Wallis.

programme.title: Mafias Greatest Hits
programme.desc: The chilling secrets of the Mafias most notorious kingpins are exposed in this compelling series. Mobster Sam Giancana was associated with the Kennedys, Frank Sinatra and the CIA.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: Based on a true story, fugitive Sam Sheppard is found guilty of killing his wife despite his pleas of innocence.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: A body is hauled out of the English Channel, however it is still very much alive and raising a family.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: A highly regarded officer is suspected in the disappearance of several soldiers.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: A mother of two is charged after placing a 911 call stating her two sons have been stabbed by an intruder.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: Woman found dead and her husband who was drunk that evening confesses to her murder but none of the evidence points to him.

programme.title: True Crime Scene
programme.desc: Ted Binion at the Las Vegas casino tells his lawyer to remove his girlfriends Sandys name from his the will if she doesnt kill him tonight.

programme.title: Wayne Couzens: Killer in Plain Sight
programme.desc: This extraordinary film reveals the shocking truth about the serving police officer who killed Sarah Everard, and asks how he was able to hide from scrutiny for so long.

programme.title: Jack the Ripper: Londons Most Notorious Killer
programme.desc: Unmasking Jack the Ripper and his evil deeds with a presentation of both factual evidence and conspiracy theories.

programme.title: Coastal Killers
programme.sub-title: Michael Peacock: The Cold Blooded Killer And The Abandoned Mine
programme.desc: Michael Peacock was having an affair with a much younger woman and had decided that he didnt want to pay for a costly divorce, so he plotted what he believed to be the perfect murder.

programme.title: Britains Forgotten Serial Killer
programme.desc: This is the chilling but little-known story of Trevor Hardy, the serial killer known as the `Beast of Manchester who murdered three teenage girls between December 1974 and March 1976.

programme.title: When Fred Met Rose
programme.desc: Documentary series examining what led Fred and Rose West to become serial killers.

programme.title: Fred and Rose: The West Murders
programme.desc: Focuses on the notorious house which the couple shared in Gloucester. Fred and Rose murdered their first three victims - Anna McFall, Charmaine West and then Rena Costello - because they `got in the way. But when they moved into 25 Cromwell Street, the couple began to kill for sexual thrills.

programme.title: Fred and Rose: The West Murders
programme.desc: Looks at how a dogged police officer, Hazel Savage, fought to have the Wests patio excavated when she became suspicious about the whereabouts of their daughter. The film also includes insights from both Fred and Rose Wests former solicitors.

programme.title: The Prosecutors: In Pursuit of Justice
programme.desc: When 27-year-old David Wahl disappears without a trace from a state park in Oregon, he leaves behind a distraught family and a host of unanswered questions. Months later and 100 miles north, Wahls body washes ashore. The death is ruled an accident, until a shocking admission by his girlfriend leads to criminal charges.

programme.title: The Prosecutors: In Pursuit of Justice
programme.desc: The disappearance of seven-year-old Amanda Brown from her Florida home sparks a massive search effort by dozens of law enforcement agencies and hundreds of concerned citizens. While the search for the missing girl continues, investigators look into the history of the last man to see her alive, and make a chilling discovery.

programme.title: The Prosecutors: In Pursuit of Justice
programme.desc: The community of Dover, Delaware, is shocked when parts of a human body turn up across the city. Authorities determine it is one victim, but forensics experts struggle to identify the person while a hunt for the killer is conducted.

programme.title: The Prosecutors: In Pursuit of Justice
programme.desc: In 1992, gang activity escalates in Chicago; with local law enforcement unable to curb the growing drug trade, a multi-agency task force headed by Assistant U.S. Attorney Ron Safer focuses on one of the citys most vicious street gangs.

programme.title: The Prosecutors: In Pursuit of Justice
programme.desc: For many teenagers, their first jobs are at fast-food restaurants. Parents feel that these are safe environments for their children. That image is shattered when seven employees of three different fast-food restaurants in the Nashville, Tennessee area are murdered over a two-month period in 1997. Months of investigation reveal a Texas parolee as the prime suspect.

programme.title: The Prosecutors: In Pursuit of Justice
programme.desc: A community is horrified when seven trick-or-treaters in Pasadena, California are sprayed with gunfire on Halloween night in 1993, leaving three young boys dead. Investigators rely on forensic clues, eyewitness accounts, and street informants to find the killers, three gang members bent on avenging a friends death.

programme.title: The Prosecutors: In Pursuit of Justice
programme.desc: An evening of pick-up basketball in Houston turns tragic when prosecutor Gil Epstein is murdered in a community center parking lot. Investigators depend on anonymous tips and hazy eyewitness accounts to track down a killer. District Attorney Chuck Rosenthal leads a prosecution team whose chances for a conviction are put in jeopardy by a single holdout juror.

programme.title: The Prosecutors: In Pursuit of Justice
programme.desc: When the death rate at a rural Indiana hospital skyrockets, an investigation implicates a nurse, Orville Lynn Majors. State Police are left with a daunting task. In order to conclude that Majors is a serial killer, police first have to first figure out if the deaths were murders and then determine how he killed the patients.

programme.title: 72 Hours: True Crime
programme.desc: A woman is found naked and beaten to death in her home.

programme.title: Coastal Killers
programme.sub-title: Michael Peacock: The Cold Blooded Killer And The Abandoned Mine
programme.desc: Michael Peacock was having an affair with a much younger woman and had decided that he didnt want to pay for a costly divorce, so he plotted what he believed to be the perfect murder.

programme.title: Britains Forgotten Serial Killer
programme.desc: This is the chilling but little-known story of Trevor Hardy, the serial killer known as the `Beast of Manchester who murdered three teenage girls between December 1974 and March 1976.

programme.title: When Fred Met Rose
programme.desc: Documentary series examining what led Fred and Rose West to become serial killers.

programme.title: When Fred Met Rose
programme.desc: Documentary series examining what led Fred and Rose West to become serial killers.

programme.title: Hidden Spain
programme.desc: Spain is found at the crossroads between the Mediterranean and the Atlantic, resulting in diverse landscapes, ancient cultures and magnificent wildlife.

programme.title: Hidden Australia
programme.desc: Australia is known for its sun-kissed beaches, however the 2,500 miles between the coasts are made up of a patchwork of contrasting landscapes, such as tropical rainforests, snow-capped mountains, dry woodlands, tropical reefs, and hostile desert.

programme.title: Addos Elephants
programme.desc: Savannah elephants thrive in South Africas Addo Elephant National Park.

programme.title: Lioness in Exile
programme.desc: Manyari takes an unusual step when she takes her young cubs away from the sanctuary of the clan.

programme.title: Big Cats of the Serengeti
programme.desc: Cameras reveal the last secrets of Africas largest wild lions by following two prides.

programme.title: A Genets Tale
programme.desc: In a hidden corner of the Mediterranean forest, three young genet cubs begin to discover the world. The little female leaves her family. She will find beautiful scenes, but a daunting reality is waiting. The common genet is the only genet in Europe.

programme.title: Lionfish: The New Pirates of the Caribbean
programme.desc: Armed with 18 venomous spines, the lionfish hunts, invisible to its prey, in packs. Shedding light on the fascinating behaviour that let the lionfish conquer foreign waters, and by doing so threaten entire ecosystems.

programme.title: Hamiltons Journey
programme.desc: For the first time, a team of filmmakers has managed to observe a manatee for three years. His name is Hamilton and lives in Florida.

programme.title: Blue World
programme.desc: Majestic and unique creatures call the underground world of Palm Beach, Fla., home; oceanic secrets can be found up close and personal across the Great Barrier Reef.

programme.title: Blue World
programme.sub-title: Sully the Pilot Whale and Cleaning Stations
programme.desc: A stranded pilot whale is rescued by the staff of the Curacao Sea Aquarium; animals have parasites and dead skin removed at cleaning stations.

programme.title: Blue World
programme.desc: Diving with sea lions in the Galapagos Islands; training sea lions at the Mystic Aquarium in Connecticut; humpback whales in Silver Bank, Dominican Republic.

programme.title: Blue World
programme.sub-title: Jake Learns to Dive and Curacao Dolphins
programme.desc: Thirteen-year-old Jake explores the underwater world of Grand Cayman Island with Jonathan; dolphin programme at the Curacao Sea Aquarium.

programme.title: Adopted by Dolphins
programme.desc: Swiss biologist Angela Ziltener and Michael Stadermann study dolphins by diving with them and learning about their behaviour close up.

programme.title: Red Panda: Worlds Cutest Animal
programme.desc: Spends a year with a red panda named Tashi in Indian forests below the Himalayas.

programme.title: Predators of a Different Kind: In the World of the Unknown Sharks
programme.desc: With the aid of modern technology, revealing the unknown behaviour of some unusual species of shark: lemon sharks and their white-tip reef counterparts. The journey starts off in the turquoise-blue waters of the Grand Bahama Bank.

programme.title: Oceanic Oases: Mysterious Hotspots of Life
programme.desc: Countless marine animals frolic on the steep slopes of underwater volcanoes. In many cases, several are rare species not found elsewhere. This is in part due to the fact that plankton clings to the wall and provides the fish with food in abundance.

programme.title: New Life: Born to Swim
programme.desc: Exploring how wildlife become expert swimmers to find food and escape danger.

programme.title: New Life: Born to Run
programme.desc: In a land of dangers, plant-eating animals are always at risk, and never more so than at the time of their birth. Out on the open they are vulnerable and there are few places to hide.

programme.title: Forests of Our Earth
programme.desc: A look at how forests execute a variety of survival strategies in different regions of the planet.

programme.title: Mantises: Living With Cannibals
programme.desc: Stealthy mantis hunts in Kaiserstuhl vineyards; mating season brings deadly survival challenges.

programme.title: Home of the Seals: on Brittanys Wild
programme.desc: Some have called the coast of Brittany the Riviera of the North; the many coves with white sandy beaches appear to be idyllic, but the Atlantic in the northwest of France is rarely calm.

programme.title: Bear Essentials
programme.desc: We follow three young bears of different species - polar bears, brown bears and sloth bears (like Baloo, the Jungle Book Bear) - that inhabit dramatically varied landscapes as they find their way in the world. We look at how their mothers teach them the skills they need to survive and watch the many dangers they face.

programme.title: Animal Homes
programme.desc: Animals make use of brilliant and extraordinary specialist building skills to construct the perfect home.

programme.title: Blue World
programme.desc: Majestic and unique creatures call the underground world of Palm Beach, Fla., home; oceanic secrets can be found up close and personal across the Great Barrier Reef.

programme.title: Blue World

programme.title: Blue World
programme.sub-title: Jake Learns to Dive and Curacao Dolphins

programme.title: Adopted by Dolphins
programme.desc: Swiss biologist Angela Ziltener and Michael Stadermann study dolphins by diving with them and learning about their behaviour close up.

programme.title: Adopted by Dolphins
programme.desc: Swiss biologist Angela Ziltener and Michael Stadermann study dolphins by diving with them and learning about their behaviour close up.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew Pritchard, a Welsh antique dealer travels the length and breadth of the United Kingdom, searching old mansions and scrap yards to hunt down architectural treasures, in order to restore them to their former glory.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew Pritchard, a Welsh antique dealer travels the length and breadth of the United Kingdom, searching old mansions and scrap yards to hunt down architectural treasures, in order to restore them to their former glory.

programme.title: Fantasy Homes by the Sea
programme.desc: A motorbike-mad couple want to move to the Isle of Man on a budget of £600,000. They need plenty of garage space for their bikes and a property with holiday-let potential.

programme.title: Cruise TV with LoveitBookit
programme.desc: A definitive one-stop shop for all things cruise, bringing you the hottest destinations and the latest deals to ensure that your next cruise ship holiday is your greatest one yet.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: After tying the knot last year, a super active and self-employed couple now desire a new rural backdrop for their married life and business. Jules Hudson is helping them find that base in the Welsh Borders, with a budget of £365k. Whilst in the region, Jules also visits a timber-frame manufacturer in Herefordshire who is keeping alive the tradition of heritage properties associated with the area.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew and Tee travel to four extraordinary locations across Northern Italy, including the countrys most famed antique markets and a dreamy Tuscan villa.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew and Tee go hunting for antiques in the homes of fellow dealers. Tucked away in the Cotswolds, Drew finds an ancient Greek masterpiece from 100BC.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew encounters thrilling examples by three design titans who shaped the classic Georgian-and-Victorian look: Sir John Soane, Augustus Pugin and William Morris.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Philip Serrell embark on the penultimate leg of their journey, which begins in the village of Amble in Northumberland and finishes at auction in Carlisle in Cumbria.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Anita Manning and Philip Serrell begin the final leg of their journey in Castle Douglas in Kirkcudbrightshire, heading to a tense final auction in Crooklands in the Lake District.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Today in the Repair Shop, Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life. First to arrive is a delicately carved set of clay sculptures, for the attention of ceramics expert Kirsten Ramsey. Owner Jackie treasures the figurines, lovingly crafted by her older brother Andrew who tragically died in an accident at the tender age of 18. After over 40 years on display, the unglazed figurines are crumbling and in dire need of repair.

programme.title: The Derbyshire Auction House
programme.desc: Irita Marriott, one of the few women in the country to helm an auction house, and her team use their expertise to launch Britains newest saleroom.

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Two incredible stories of people desperate to find missing family: a mother living with a heart-breaking decision made when she was still a child to give up her son for adoption - and a woman on a quest to solve a family mystery and find her sisters.

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: The incredible stories of two people who are trying to track down missing relatives, including the heart-breaking story of Jacinta Hickey who wanted to find her younger brother Paul, and after 20 years of searching, Judy Kenyons hopes of finding her mother and her sister have reached a dead end, but Long Lost Family have a surprise in store for her.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Cancer survivor Claire wants to downsize to free up some cash and have fun, and Clive needs funding for his mums care home costs.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Theres a piratical mood for antiques experts Raj Bisram and Irita Marriott as they set sail on their final outing together. There are shops to plunder and booty to be bought as they explore the very tip of the Cornish peninsula. And with the competition being so close at this point, the pressure is on to find that auction-winning item. Raj makes some big finds in one of the tiniest shops of the trip and pins his hopes on a risky item that may or may not be highly collectable.

programme.title: Fixer to Fabulous

programme.title: Auction
programme.desc: Featuring art masterpieces and other luxury items that are sold to the highest bidder at some of the worlds most prestigious auction houses. Featured items include antique cars, sculptures, rare musical instruments, jewellery and artefacts.

programme.title: Auction
programme.desc: At Sothebys in New York an 1897 painting by Gustav Klimt has surfaced after years in a private collection. Plus a work from the later years of Renoir is auctioned.

programme.title: Tchaikovsky, Swan Lake

programme.title: Watercolour Challenge

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A tourist on a transatlantic liner stakes his all on one last, desperate gamble.

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: An antiques dealer (Robert H. Harris) decides his art objects are too beautiful to sell. With Louise Larabee.

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Presents
programme.desc: An insecure woman (Phyllis Thaxter) who cant control her drinking loses control at a cocktail party.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: Explore the life of the highly influential director credited as the inventor of the Italian Western genre. His films include Once Upon a Time In America.

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: An inside look at the life and career of Emmy and Golden Globe-winning actress Jean Simmons.

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A neurotic woman with a fear of not being punctual is tormented by her husband on a troubled trip to America.

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: A story of greed and deceit in which an art collector is drawn into a battle with two glamorous women.
